page.title: Motorcycle Oil Grades Explained
page.summary: Understanding SAE viscosity ratings, JASO MA/MA2, and API classifications.
page.content:
SAE Viscosity

The first number (e.g. 10W-40) indicates cold-start viscosity. The W means Winter. Lower numbers flow better in cold conditions.

The second number (e.g. 10W-40) indicates viscosity at operating temperature. Higher numbers provide thicker protection at high heat.

JASO Certification

  • JASO MA — Standard for wet-clutch motorcycles. No friction modifiers that cause clutch slip.
  • JASO MA2 — Higher friction performance for modern bikes. Preferred for most current motorcycles.
  • JASO MB — For scooters with CVT. Not suitable for manual-clutch bikes.

API Rating

Look for API SG or higher. Avoid automotive oils with energy-conserving labels — they contain friction modifiers harmful to wet clutches.
